creator: dewayne@heuristicsystems.com.au
text:
(In reply to Franco Fichtner from comment #6)
Thank-you for the additional input.  I'd make two suggestions:
a) open a separate PR as this one, deals with compilation failure using gcc
b) remove WITHOUT_CLANG_FULL=1.  Though I make this statement from my reading of "man src.conf" and the fact that net-snmp compiled with errors when I raised this PR.  (which has since "gone away")

Perhaps, if you're getting desperate, try using lang/gcc.  A quick way is to add "USE_GCC= any" to the net-snmp/Makefile.  I'd also suggest moving to 10.2 a lot of useful fixes were applied, including updates to pf. :)
